identificationModule.briefTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Jia Shen Tablets in Chronic Heart Failure
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind and Multi-Centre Phase II Clinical Trial：Efficacy and Safety of Jia Shen Tablets in the Treatment of Coronary Heart Disease Complicating Chronic Heart Failure (Syndrome of Yang Qi Deficiency With Blood Stasis)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Evaluate the efficacy and safety of Jia Shen Tablets in patients with Chronic Heart Failure.

armGroups.label: Low dose group
interventionNames: Drug: Low dose Jia Shen Tablet

armGroups.label: High dose group
interventionNames: Drug: High dose Jia Shen Tablet

interventions.name: Jia Shen Tablet Placebo
interventions.description: Jia Shen Tablet Placebo contains 4 placebo tablets (0.47 g per placebo tablet),take orally, 2 times a day for 12 weeks

interventions.name: Low dose Jia Shen Tablet
interventions.description: Low dose Jia Shen Tablet contains 2 tablets (0.47 g per tablet) and 2 placebo tablets (0.47 g per placebo tablet),take orally, 2 times a day for 12 weeks

interventions.name: High dose Jia Shen Tablet
interventions.description: High dose Jia Shen Tablet contains 4 tablets (0.47 g per tablet),take orally, 2 times a day for 12 weeks

primaryOutcomes.measure: 6-Minutes-Walking-Distance (6MWD): the change from baseline to Week 12.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: 6-Minutes-Walking-Distance (6MWD): the change from baseline to Week 6.
NT-proBNP: the change from baseline to Week 6 and 12.
NT-proBNP: the proportion of patients demonstrating a decrease in NT-proBNP level of at least 30%.

Echocardiogram: Measurements will be made of the left ventricle's end diastolic diameter (LVEDD), end diastolic volume (LVEDV), end systolic volume (LVESV), left ventricular ejection fraction（LVEF）, stroke volume (SV), and cardiac output (CO).
NYHA functional classification: Measure the percentage of participants whose NYHA functional classification is improved/worsened/unchanged from baseline to Week 6 and 12.
MLHFQ Score: the change from baseline to Week 6 and 12.
secondaryOutcomes.description: The MLHFQ consists of 21 elements representing varying degrees of impact of HF on health related quality of life. Each item in this questionnaire is scored from 0 (none) to 5 (very much), and the total item score is from 0 (none) to 105 (very much).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Traditional Chinese Medicine（TCM） Syndrome Score: change from baseline to Week 6 and 12.
secondaryOutcomes.description: There were 3 primary symptoms and 4 secondary symptoms. The state of the tongue and pulse is used to evaluate and score TCM symptoms. The standard of TCM primary symptoms scored as without 0, light 2, medium 4, heavy 6 points. The standard of TCM secondary symptoms scored as without 0, light 1, medium 2, heavy 3 points. Tongue picture and pulse condition did not count.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Dyspnea, weakness and edema: the rate of disappearance of the symptoms (dyspnea, weakness, and edema) from baseline to Week 6 and 12.

Cardiovascular Adverse Events: Incidence of Cardiovascular AEs（cardiovascular death, hospitalization for worsening heart failure, hospitalization for nonfatal myocardial infarction, hospitalization for nonfatal stroke）.

locations.facility: The First Affiliated Hospital of Tianjin University of traditional Chinese
